title: Beef Tagine with Xenial Flair
description: A hearty and aromatic beef tagine dish infused with the warm spices of the Maghreb, featuring a blend of xenial and tunisian flavors.

ingredients.name: xenial spice blend
ingredients.notes: Available at most Middle Eastern markets or online

ingredients.name: onion
ingredients.notes: Yellow or white onion works best

ingredients.name: garlic
ingredients.notes: Use fresh for the best flavor

ingredients.name: tagine spice blend
ingredients.notes: Available at most North African markets or online

ingredients.name: cumin
ingredients.notes: Ground or whole cumin seeds work well

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher or sea salt for the best flavor

instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a large clay or ceramic tagine pot over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the beef and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the xenial spice blend, tagine spice blend, cumin, cinnamon, salt, and chicken broth.
Stir to combine, then cover the pot with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the oven and cook at 300°F (150°C) for 2 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the beef and sauce over couscous or rice.
Garnish with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
